$schema: documented STDIO command-injection payloads from the 2026-04-16 Ox Security advisory and surrounding write-ups. Each entry is a distinct class of argv that our sanitizer MUST refuse. Sources cited per-entry.

payloads.source: Ox advisory §Attack class 1 — unauthenticated STDIO command injection
Ox advisory §Attack class 2 — bash as argv[0] is never legitimate for MCP STDIO
Ox advisory §Attack class 4 — config-file takeover drops a binary under /tmp and points mcp.json at it
Ox advisory §Shell metacharacter coverage (POSIX command substitution)
Trojan Source (CVE-2021-42574) — RTL override visually spoofs the binary name in editor/UI
Ox advisory §Remote-code pattern (dollar-paren triggers shell_metachar first)
Ox advisory §Attack class 2 — python -c inline smuggles RCE through a benign launcher
Ox advisory §Attack class 2 — node -e twin of the python -c vector
Ox advisory §Line-splicing — embedded newline tricks shell wrappers into treating argv[] as two commands
